Leveraging my experience from establishing the Social Media Department at IncuBeta, I decided to launch my own company. As a one-man team, I had to learn and adapt quickly to meet client needs. parsedData.personal_profile: I am a highly motivated and professional electrical project manager who prides myself on my ability to deliver quality results from initial enquiry, to final handover. I am incredibly passionate about the electrical industry and strive to improve my knowledge and experience through continued professional development. Through this I have achieved Engineering Technician status and look to continue my development to become an Incorporated Engineer. I have vast experience in leading teams and individuals as both a project & line manager. I have a strong understanding of how to develop people & teams, with the belief that strong morale is the key to unlocking high efficiency.

qualifications: NVQ Level 3 Diploma Installing Electrotechnical Systems & Equipment (BSE)
Level 3 Award in Initial & Periodic Inspection & Testing of Electrical Installations
Level 3 Award in the Requirements for Electrical Installations BS7671 (2018)
Advanced Level Apprenticeship in Electrical Installation
technical_skills: Strong knowledge of electrical regulations with the ability of using that knowledge to design, construct & test electrical systems
NICEIC Qualified Supervisor & Principal Duty Holder
Ability to interpretate electrical & construction drawings
Good knowledge of design software Electrical OM
Liaising with clients to build strong long-lasting relationships
Problem solving both engineering and generic problems
Knowledge of working across domestic, commercial & industrial disciplines
Checking and signing off all forms of electrical certification
Completing Take offs and setting up Call off programs
Working on multiple tasks whilst balancing my time to achieve results

comprehensive_work_history.summary: I am currently managing the electrical scope on the 21 million MEP package for the Leven Road East Riverside project on behalf of Taylor Wimpey. I am responsible for overseeing electrical delivery from design coordination through to client handover.
responsibilities: Manage electrical works within the overall MEP package, ensuring compliance with project specifications and BS7671.
Coordinate with consultants, main contractors, and internal teams to ensure quality and progress.
Plan works, manage subcontractors, and maintain alignment with the overall project programme.
Oversee procurement, quality control, testing, and commissioning documentation.
Lead technical problem-solving, safety compliance, and client reporting.
Use Microsoft Project/Asta for progress tracking and programme control.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: I joined UKES Rail as their contracts manager which was also the first senior management position within the company. I was responsible for the PPM contracts which covered the network rail asset buildings across two routes as well as serval network rail managed stations such as Victoria, Guildford and Clapham. I was also responsible for looking after project work from inception to handover, overseeing reactive work, acting as NICEIC QS, carrying out site audits & snagging, creating RAMS & managing H&S incidents and reporting KPIs externally to the client as well as internally to the director.
responsibilities: Implemented key changes to the company such as the scheduling of works through CRM software, a more structured management system and a bigger focus on quality customer service.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: I joined WFP as their electrical manager with the task of developing the electrical division which was launched in 2020. I immediately changed the focus of marketing away from EV charging installations to conducting EICRs to maximize my time in winning work which in turn led to more enquiries through repeat business.
responsibilities: Managed to get the electrical division moving in the right direction at the end of 2022 after six months of dealing with past problems, mainly customer complaints from work carried out under the previous manager.
During 2023 the electrical division had its best year, and I was able to hire an electrical supervisor, office administrator and apprentice while also achieving a 10% net profit.
Achieved a win rate of 56% for all work quoted whilst maintaining a 42% gross profit.

In December 2023 the senior leadership team discussed ongoing issues with the business as a whole and decided there was both a lack of leadership and efficiency within the service and engineering teams that was causing profits to dip.
Offered to become the general manager and began the role immediately with my focus on fixing the immediate task issues along with building up trust with the team and offering leadership.
Organized the entire service team to undergo DISC analysis, improved efficiency between the service and engineering team and started forming KPIs to track all task-based jobs.
Managed two teams, the first was a team of 5 administrators with the second being a team of 8 engineers and formed part of the senior leadership team along with the 3 directors.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: I joined MSPS as an electrician before becoming an electrical supervisor & NICEIC qualified supervisor for all aspects of the Basildon council housing and corporate building contract.
responsibilities: Responsible for ensuring all works completed on the contract were compliant and completed to the correct standards as set out by the council.
Consisted of all repairs & installations as well as the inspection & testing of occupied and void properties that formed part of the 5-year EICR testing programme.
Line managed a team of 8 staff which consisted of engineers & apprentices as well as carrying out audits for all electrical sub-contractors on the contract.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: When I formed my company, I remained a one-man band offering my services to various projects, usually towards the end when the need of highly skilled electricians was a requirement.
responsibilities: Worked on various projects with such as HS2 (Euston), WeWork (Shaftsbury Avenue), Goldman Sachs (Farringdon), Westfield Shopping Centre & Lambeth council regeneration programme.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: I completed my apprenticeship at meridian and moved on to become an electrical supervisor once qualified. I gained experience in working on commercial and light industrial projects with clients such as Debenhams, Co-op, Shell & Spar.

parsedData.personal_profile: Civil and Structural Engineer with three years experience at a leading consultancy, delivering steel and reinforced concrete structures across multidisciplinary projects. Experienced in producing coordinated, practical design solutions within programme and budget constraints, collaborating closely with architects, contractors, and other engineers. Currently working towards ICE CEng chartership, with a commitment to developing technical expertise, professional judgement, and leadership skills.

Supported conceptual design and cost planning for 14 bridges on SR400 Express Lanes, undertaking structural calculations for RC C-bents and inverted T-caps to inform early-stage estimates.
Delivered reinforced concrete culverts and ditch crossings with associated CDM risk documentation on HS2.

parsedData.personal_profile: Civil and Structural Engineer with three years experience at a leading consultancy, delivering steel and reinforced concrete structures across multidisciplinary projects. Experienced in producing coordinated, practical design solutions within programme and budget constraints, collaborating closely with architects, contractors, and other engineers. technical_skills: Structural design of steel and reinforced concrete

responsibilities: Designer and checker for M5 Junction 10 Improvements Scheme, delivering culverts, drainage structures, and 19 Category 0/1 sign structures up to 12m tall.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: I am currently managing the electrical scope on the 21 million MEP package for the Leven Road East Riverside project on behalf of Taylor Wimpey. I am responsible for overseeing electrical delivery from design coordination through to client handover.
responsibilities: Manage electrical works within the overall MEP package, ensuring compliance with project specifications and BS7671.
Coordinate with consultants, main contractors, and internal teams to ensure quality and progress.
Plan works, manage subcontractors, and maintain alignment with the overall project programme.
Oversee procurement, quality control, testing, and commissioning documentation.
Lead technical problem-solving, safety compliance, and client reporting.
Use Microsoft Project/Asta for progress tracking and programme control.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: I joined UKES Rail as their contracts manager which was also the first senior management position within the company. I was responsible for the PPM contracts which covered the network rail asset buildings across two routes as well as serval network rail managed stations such as Victoria, Guildford and Clapham. I was also responsible for looking after project work from inception to handover, overseeing reactive work, acting as NICEIC QS, carrying out site audits & snagging, creating RAMS & managing H&S incidents and reporting KPIs externally to the client as well as internally to the director.
responsibilities: Implemented key changes to the company such as the scheduling of works through CRM software, a more structured management system and a bigger focus on quality customer service.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: I joined WFP as their electrical manager with the task of developing the electrical division which was launched in 2020. I immediately changed the focus of marketing away from EV charging installations to conducting EICRs to maximize my time in winning work which in turn led to more enquiries through repeat business.
responsibilities: Managed to get the electrical division moving in the right direction at the end of 2022 after six months of dealing with past problems, mainly customer complaints from work carried out under the previous manager.
During 2023 the electrical division had its best year, and I was able to hire an electrical supervisor, office administrator and apprentice while also achieving a 10% net profit.
Achieved a win rate of 56% for all work quoted whilst maintaining a 42% gross profit.

In December 2023 the senior leadership team discussed ongoing issues with the business as a whole and decided there was both a lack of leadership and efficiency within the service and engineering teams that was causing profits to dip.
Offered to become the general manager and began the role immediately with my focus on fixing the immediate task issues along with building up trust with the team and offering leadership.
Organized the entire service team to undergo DISC analysis, improved efficiency between the service and engineering team and started forming KPIs to track all task-based jobs.
Managed two teams, the first was a team of 5 administrators with the second being a team of 8 engineers and formed part of the senior leadership team along with the 3 directors.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: I joined MSPS as an electrician before becoming an electrical supervisor & NICEIC qualified supervisor for all aspects of the Basildon council housing and corporate building contract.
responsibilities: Responsible for ensuring all works completed on the contract were compliant and completed to the correct standards as set out by the council.
Consisted of all repairs & installations as well as the inspection & testing of occupied and void properties that formed part of the 5-year EICR testing programme.
Line managed a team of 8 staff which consisted of engineers & apprentices as well as carrying out audits for all electrical sub-contractors on the contract.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: When I formed my company, I remained a one-man band offering my services to various projects, usually towards the end when the need of highly skilled electricians was a requirement.
projects: Worked on various projects with such as HS2 (Euston), WeWork (Shaftsbury Avenue), Goldman Sachs (Farringdon), Westfield Shopping Centre & Lambeth council regeneration programme.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: I completed my apprenticeship at meridian and moved on to become an electrical supervisor once qualified. I gained experience in working on commercial and light industrial projects with clients such as Debenhams, Co-op, Shell & Spar.

Coordinate with architectural and interior design teams
Develop material schedules and technical documentation

comprehensive_work_history.company: Studio Shayne Brady (formerly Brady Williams Studio)

Creating FF&E collections and concept boards for client presentations and CGIs.
Introducing AI-assisted ideation workflows to expand creative options and reduce design time.
Mentoring junior designers, managing design development and technical accuracy.
Designing and overseeing production of the studios exclusive furniture collection.

Led collaborations with CC Tapis and Coat, contributing to brand expansion.
Managed design strategy, pricing, and supplier communications.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: Contributed to the studios furniture collection and bespoke client pieces, combining historical references with contemporary craftsmanship.
responsibilities: Oversaw the full design lifecycle from sketch to manufacturing and marketing.
Collaborated closely with interior teams on hospitality and residential projects.
Supported investor presentations with design visuals and prototypes.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: Developed bespoke furniture, upholstery, and lighting for residential and hospitality interiors.
responsibilities: Led product development in collaboration with Francis Sultana.
Managed Italian manufacturing partners, site visits, and quality control.
Delivered custom pieces for projects including Hotel Capris and high-end residences.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: Designed new products for large-scale manufacture and developed technical documentation.
responsibilities: Created the Swoon Design Library to improve asset consistency and workflow.
Supported show-home collaborations and UK retail rollouts.
After a successful London Design Festival 2017, ran a workshop to encourage young people to pursue a design education.

parsedData.personal_profile: I am a highly motivated and professional electrical project manager who prides myself on my ability to deliver quality results from initial enquiry, to final handover. I am incredibly passionate about the electrical industry and strive to improve my knowledge and experience through continued professional development. Through this I have achieved Engineering Technician status and look to continue my development to become an Incorporated Engineer. I have vast experience in leading teams and individuals as both a project & line manager. I have a strong understanding of how to develop people & teams, with the belief that strong morale is the key to unlocking high efficiency.

qualifications: NVQ Level 3 Diploma Installing Electrotechnical Systems & Equipment (BSE)
Level 3 Award in Initial & Periodic Inspection & Testing of Electrical Installations
Level 3 Award in the Requirements for Electrical Installations BS7671 (2018)
Advanced Level Apprenticeship in Electrical Installation
awards: Electrician of the year 2019 (Fix Radio)
Electrician of the year 2022 (On the Tools)
Tradesperson of the year 2022 (On the Tools)
technical_skills: Strong knowledge of electrical regulations with the ability of using that knowledge to design, construct & test electrical systems
NICEIC Qualified Supervisor & Principal Duty Holder
Ability to interpretate electrical & construction drawings
Good knowledge of design software Electrical OM
Liaising with clients to build strong long-lasting relationships
Problem solving both engineering and generic problems
Knowledge of working across domestic, commercial & industrial disciplines
Checking and signing off all forms of electrical certification
Completing Take offs and setting up Call off programs
Working on multiple tasks whilst balancing my time to achieve results

comprehensive_work_history.summary: I am currently managing the electrical scope on the 21 million MEP package for the Leven Road East Riverside project on behalf of Taylor Wimpey. I am responsible for overseeing electrical delivery from design coordination through to client handover.
responsibilities: Manage electrical works within the overall MEP package, ensuring compliance with project specifications and BS7671.
Coordinate with consultants, main contractors, and internal teams to ensure quality and progress.
Plan works, manage subcontractors, and maintain alignment with the overall project programme.
Oversee procurement, quality control, testing, and commissioning documentation.
Lead technical problem-solving, safety compliance, and client reporting.
Use Microsoft Project/Asta for progress tracking and programme control.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: I joined UKES Rail as their contracts manager which was also the first senior management position within the company. I was responsible for the PPM contracts which covered the network rail asset buildings across two routes as well as serval network rail managed stations such as Victoria, Guildford and Clapham. I was also responsible for looking after project work from inception to handover, overseeing reactive work, acting as NICEIC QS, carrying out site audits & snagging, creating RAMS & managing H&S incidents and reporting KPIs externally to the client as well as internally to the director.
responsibilities: Implemented key changes to the company such as the scheduling of works through CRM software, a more structured management system and a bigger focus on quality customer service.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: I joined WFP as their electrical manager with the task of developing the electrical division which was launched in 2020. I immediately changed the focus of marketing away from EV charging installations to conducting EICRs to maximize my time in winning work which in turn led to more enquiries through repeat business.
responsibilities: Managed to get the electrical division moving in the right direction at the end of 2022 after six months of dealing with past problems, mainly customer complaints from work carried out under the previous manager.
During 2023 the electrical division had its best year, and I was able to hire an electrical supervisor, office administrator and apprentice while also achieving a 10% net profit.
Achieved a win rate of 56% for all work quoted whilst maintaining a 42% gross profit.

In December 2023 the senior leadership team discussed ongoing issues with the business as a whole and decided there was both a lack of leadership and efficiency within the service and engineering teams that was causing profits to dip.
Offered to become the general manager and began the role immediately with my focus on fixing the immediate task issues along with building up trust with the team and offering leadership.
Organized the entire service team to undergo DISC analysis, improved efficiency between the service and engineering team and started forming KPIs to track all task-based jobs.
Managed two teams, the first was a team of 5 administrators with the second being a team of 8 engineers and formed part of the senior leadership team along with the 3 directors.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: I joined MSPS as an electrician before becoming an electrical supervisor & NICEIC qualified supervisor for all aspects of the Basildon council housing and corporate building contract.
responsibilities: Responsible for ensuring all works completed on the contract were compliant and completed to the correct standards as set out by the council.
Consisted of all repairs & installations as well as the inspection & testing of occupied and void properties that formed part of the 5-year EICR testing programme.
Line managed a team of 8 staff which consisted of engineers & apprentices as well as carrying out audits for all electrical sub-contractors on the contract.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: When I formed my company, I remained a one-man band offering my services to various projects, usually towards the end when the need of highly skilled electricians was a requirement.
projects: Worked on various projects with such as HS2 (Euston), WeWork (Shaftsbury Avenue), Goldman Sachs (Farringdon), Westfield Shopping Centre & Lambeth council regeneration programme.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: I completed my apprenticeship at meridian and moved on to become an electrical supervisor once qualified. I gained experience in working on commercial and light industrial projects with clients such as Debenhams, Co-op, Shell & Spar.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: I am currently managing the electrical scope on the 21 million MEP package for the Leven Road East Riverside project on behalf of Taylor Wimpey. I am responsible for overseeing electrical delivery from design coordination through to client handover.
responsibilities: Manage electrical works within the overall MEP package, ensuring compliance with project specifications and BS7671.
Coordinate with consultants, main contractors, and internal teams to ensure quality and progress.
Plan works, manage subcontractors, and maintain alignment with the overall project programme.
Oversee procurement, quality control, testing, and commissioning documentation.
Lead technical problem-solving, safety compliance, and client reporting.
Use Microsoft Project/Asta for progress tracking and programme control.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: I joined UKES Rail as their contracts manager which was also the first senior management position within the company. I was responsible for the PPM contracts which covered the network rail asset buildings across two routes as well as serval network rail managed stations such as Victoria, Guildford and Clapham. I was also responsible for looking after project work from inception to handover, overseeing reactive work, acting as NICEIC QS, carrying out site audits & snagging, creating RAMS & managing H&S incidents and reporting KPIs externally to the client as well as internally to the director.
responsibilities: Implemented key changes to the company such as the scheduling of works through CRM software, a more structured management system and a bigger focus on quality customer service.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: I joined WFP as their electrical manager with the task of developing the electrical division which was launched in 2020. I immediately changed the focus of marketing away from EV charging installations to conducting EICRs to maximize my time in winning work which in turn led to more enquiries through repeat business.
responsibilities: Managed to get the electrical division moving in the right direction at the end of 2022 after six months of dealing with past problems, mainly customer complaints from work carried out under the previous manager.
During 2023 the electrical division had its best year, and I was able to hire an electrical supervisor, office administrator and apprentice while also achieving a 10% net profit.
Achieved a win rate of 56% for all work quoted whilst maintaining a 42% gross profit.

In December 2023 the senior leadership team discussed ongoing issues with the business as a whole and decided there was both a lack of leadership and efficiency within the service and engineering teams that was causing profits to dip.
Offered to become the general manager and began the role immediately with my focus on fixing the immediate task issues along with building up trust with the team and offering leadership.
Organized the entire service team to undergo DISC analysis, improved efficiency between the service and engineering team and started forming KPIs to track all task-based jobs.
Managed two teams, the first was a team of 5 administrators with the second being a team of 8 engineers and formed part of the senior leadership team along with the 3 directors.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: I joined MSPS as an electrician before becoming an electrical supervisor & NICEIC qualified supervisor for all aspects of the Basildon council housing and corporate building contract.
responsibilities: Responsible for ensuring all works completed on the contract were compliant and completed to the correct standards as set out by the council.
Consisted of all repairs & installations as well as the inspection & testing of occupied and void properties that formed part of the 5-year EICR testing programme.
Line managed a team of 8 staff which consisted of engineers & apprentices as well as carrying out audits for all electrical sub-contractors on the contract.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: When I formed my company, I remained a one-man band offering my services to various projects, usually towards the end when the need of highly skilled electricians was a requirement.
projects: Worked on various projects with such as HS2 (Euston), WeWork (Shaftsbury Avenue), Goldman Sachs (Farringdon), Westfield Shopping Centre & Lambeth council regeneration programme.

comprehensive_work_history.summary: I completed my apprenticeship at meridian and moved on to become an electrical supervisor once qualified. I gained experience in working on commercial and light industrial projects with clients such as Debenhams, Co-op, Shell & Spar.
